cover.title: THE OCEAN
cover.subtitle: The one who wants depth, then steps back when it starts to matter.
cover.tagline: You want something real — but the closer it gets, the more carefully you move.

profile_scores.caption: High warmth and caution, low expression — you want something real, but the closer you get, the less you say.

short_summary: You don't want a light relationship — when love matters, you become more careful, not less.
The distance people feel from you is often the sign that the relationship has become important.
What you're learning isn't how to love deeper. It's how to stay present before certainty arrives.

full_report.headline: THE OCEAN
full_report.intro: You're not someone who wants a light relationship. When you start liking someone, you get far more serious than people would guess. This report follows the pattern underneath that: why you step back when a relationship gets important, what timing it has cost you, and how love changes when you stop carrying the uncertainty alone.

chapters.title: When You Love
body: You're not someone who wants a light relationship.
Dating out of boredom, getting together out of loneliness — that's not really you.
It's actually the opposite. When you start liking someone, you get far more serious than people would guess.
So you start slow. You don't go in without certainty. You're not good at vague, undefined relationships either.
But here's the interesting part — the deeper the relationship gets, the more careful you become.
People usually get more comfortable as they get closer. You start thinking more as you get closer.
Is this person really okay. Am I giving too much. Will I end up losing myself.
So people sometimes get the wrong idea. That you like them less.
But it's actually the opposite. You're being careful because it's become more important.
chapters.profile_note: High Warmth · High Caution, Low Expression — You want something real, but the closer you get, the less you say.

chapters.title: The Pattern You Repeat
body: At first it goes well. You slowly get to know each other. Slowly get closer. Trust builds between you too.
But a certain point comes. The moment the other person wants to get even closer. Meet more often. Share more. Connect more deeply.
That's when, strangely, you take a step back.
Not because you dislike it. Not because it feels like too much, either. It's because you're scared.
Because the relationship has become too important.
So you start checking. Over and over. One more time. And one more time again.
And in that process, the other person feels like you're drifting further away.
chapters.highlight: What you lose isn't the relationship. It's the timing — lost while you wait for certainty.

chart.title: THE RETREAT CURVE
chart.description: The pattern of taking a step back the deeper the relationship gets.

chapters.title: What You Got Wrong About Yourself

items.truth: No. It's not that being alone is comfortable — it's that not being disappointed is comfortable. The two are quite different things.
items.truth_short: Being alone isn't the comfort. Avoiding disappointment is.

items.belief: Let's just check a little more.
items.truth: Hmm. In most cases, you've already seen plenty. This isn't checking — you're putting off a decision.
items.truth_short: You've usually seen enough. More checking can become delayed decision-making.

items.truth: No. You're far more emotional than you think. You just don't express it. So people assume you're fine. Even when you're genuinely upset.
items.truth_short: You feel more than people realize — you just rarely show it.

chapters.title: The Person You Keep Choosing
body: You're repeatedly drawn to independent people.
Good at their own work. Fine on their own. Someone whose life feels solid.
Because that kind of person feels safe.
But here's the problem. If you're both independent, there might be no one who actually closes the gap.
You both wait for the other one to come first.
chapters.highlight: Someone who respects you gives you space. Someone who loves you crosses that space too.

chapters.title: What Actually Changes Something
body: What you need isn't to be even more careful. You're already careful enough.
What you need is to show a little more.
More people than you think don't know what's going on inside you. Because you never say.
So this time, try saying it first, even just a little. That you were hurt. That you missed them. That you were anxious.
That's not weakness. It's connection.
chapters.action_sentence: This time, connection begins when you say what you usually carry alone.

chapters.title: Where to Go From Here
body: You know how to love deeply. You don't treat it lightly. You don't give up easily either.
But what you'll learn next is how to move even without certainty.
Relationships aren't a matter of proof. There's a stretch where, at some point, you just have to trust and go.
Once you learn that, love becomes far less lonely.
chapters.transition_note: You've spent a long time learning how to protect yourself. Now you're learning how to stay in the relationship longer.

chapters.title: The Type You Keep Falling For

items.description: Their weekends are always full on their own. They never ask you to come along, but they always leave the door open. You admire their independence. Months later, you realize neither of you ever learned how to move toward each other.
No matter what happens, they stay calm. They rarely complain. They rarely show hurt. At first it feels safe. Later, you discover you've been guessing their feelings the entire time.
You can talk for hours about life, work, and dreams. The connection feels profound. But thoughtful conversations and committed actions aren't always the same thing.
They take their time. They never rush you. It feels comfortable and respectful. But sometimes both of you keep waiting so long that the relationship never truly begins.

chapters.title: The Warning Signs You've Missed
chapters.intro_note: Most of these tend to show up when the relationship is actually getting better. Which makes them even easier to miss.

signal_strength.label: You hold back expressing how you feel, even when you like them
Your actions are slower than your feelings
The other person is getting closer while you keep double-checking
You try to handle your anxiety alone instead of explaining it

chapters.core_signal: The other person is trying to get closer, while you're trying to get more certain.

chapters.title: What a Healthy Relationship Looks Like to You

checklist.title: You can say how you feel.
You don't hide your anxiety.
checklist.description: Saying it actually brings the relationship closer.

checklist.title: You can move without certainty.
checklist.description: Love doesn't have to wait until you've gathered every last piece of proof.

chapters.title: What Happens in Your Next Relationship

sections.who_breaks_you: You're weak to people who rarely say how they feel. Because you can interpret for them instead. Understand for them. Wait for them. Endure for them. But the longer that goes on, the more the whole relationship turns into guesswork.
sections.who_steadies_you: On the other hand, there's someone who fits you well. Someone who says what they think, says when they're hurt, says when they like something. This kind of person doesn't pressure you. They reassure you. So you don't have to keep checking.
sections.same_pattern_repeats: This time, you'll feel it faster. The fondness will build. And at the same time, the urge to take a step back will rise too. If this were before, you'd have read that as a warning. This time, it might not be one. It might just be a reaction to the relationship mattering.
sections.next_signs: The sign will be the moment you want to retreat right when you actually care more. That is where the old pattern usually begins.
sections.try_this: This time, don't retreat — say it. Just once. Not after everything's sorted out. Not after you feel certain. In that exact moment. Try: "I've been a little anxious lately." or "I've been thinking a lot." Then watch how they respond.
sections.final_line: A healthy person moves closer. An unhealthy person finds your vulnerability burdensome. That reaction is the answer.

action_plan.summary: You already know how to protect yourself. What's left is learning how to stay present when the relationship starts to matter.
action_items: This week, say one small feeling before you're completely certain.
When you want to step back, ask whether the relationship is actually unsafe — or simply important.
Let one person know when you're anxious instead of solving it alone.

closing.message: You've spent a long time learning how to protect yourself. This report covered why you take a step back at the very moment you want it most — and how that pattern begins to change.
closing.cross_sell_cta: Curious what your partner's pattern looks like? See their Cosmic Blueprint.
